license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbu6inze
title: Active Avoidance protocol 01282020
author: Teresa Macheda

steps_raw.instruction: Experiment Design and Recommendations: Housing: Behavior is affected by the housing mode. Singly housed mice could perform differently than group housed mice. Blinding: Experimental groups of mice should be coded while the operator is testing the mice. Test order: Mice should be randomized in test order, to avoid confounded results if all the mice from the same experimental group are tested before all the mice from a different group. The randomization should be applied in all the cohorts tested across time. Time of testing : Time of day strongly affects performance. Testing should be conducted during the same time between days and experiments. If a study requires more animals, several cohorts should be tested. It is recommended that each cohort of mice is tested starting the same day of the week and by the same personnel to reduce the introduction of new variability in the task. Sex: Males and females should be tested in separate groups, in particular in different cohorts. If this is not possible and more than one active avoidance chamber is available make sure to dedicate one chamber for each sex.

Set up Computer setup Turn on the computer Make sure that the Avoidance system is plugged in and turned on Open the avoidance system program (GEMINI.exe) Click file-> click create a new study database Name your DATABASE making sure to leave the m-defined extension .mdb and click Open A "connecting" window will pop pup, click OK Defining a Protocol Make your protocol, click "Add Protocol Record" e.g. Protocol ID: active avoidance CS Type: House US Type: Shock Gate: Open Misc.: Assume location/ Co-Terminate CS/US Trial type: Active Acclimation (sec): 300 Accl Intrvl (sec): 30 CS Duration (sec): 10 US Duration (sec): 2 Max trial Length (sec): 12 -this will be calculated automatically Max Num Trial: 50 ITI= 30±5 sec will be generated by the software following this steps: click "Add ITI Record -> name it Min (sec): 25 Max (sec): 35 Delta (sec): 5 Quantity: 49 -click Generate Random Save protocol record Example of Active avoidance protocol How to open study database/ protocol Click file-> click open existing study database Choose your database and click open. The database contains any previously defined protocols. The protocol of your choice will be ready to be applied to your study How to save your file Click file, then “save as” How to add subjects to the experiment Once the protocol is defined, you can add subjects to your experiment. Click triangle-star and pen button on top left side (you can create a session and add subjects) Click Session Click Add session record, Fill in Session ID (e.g. name , date) Click OK Under “Protocol ID,” select the appropriate protocol Click Subjects Fill in group ID, and group order (you can just put a random #), then click add subject record If the animal ID is already saved, click Subject ID and select your subject Select the station (it is important to assign the subject to the correct station/chamber when you use more than one box) Click Preload session to trial, data will be preloaded and experiment will be able to start Click Run -> click Trial -> Type in your name in the “user login” box that pops up Now mice can be loaded in the gemini box, push the "start" button on the box, trial will start and data will be recorded

Experimental Procedure Before beginning, clean the chamber floor and walls Place bedding in the tray beneath the grid floor Place the mouse in one of the two compartments (Right or left side) Shut the door Press start on the Gemini box: after 5 minutes of habituation, the mouse will be tested in a total of 50 trials When the mouse is finished it may be returned to its home cage Clean the chamber floor and walls between each mouse in order to maintain a consistent intensity of shock released from the floor Place next mouse in the chamber and continue with steps 1-7 Repeat 1-7 every day for 5 days
Export data Click Reports Select the data you want to export under Session ID -> Click Export to Excel File Select the file -> click OK Name your file -> click open
Cleaning up Clean the chamber floor and walls Empty the chamber tray of bedding, and clean the drawer Return mice to their home rooms

How to check if the chamber works properly Computer setup Turn on the computer Make sure that the Avoidance system is plugged in and turned on Open the avoidance system program ( GEMINI.exe ) Click Run -> click Diagnostics Click on the chamber controls to evaluate if the chamber is working properly

Setting up the gemini box Active Avoidance Maintenance: Each testing day, the lights and shock should be tested before the start of each testing session. Active Avoidance box should be cleaned between subjects, in particular the chamber floor. The presence of urine will increase the shock intensity experienced by the animal. Turn on the box Set up the shock intensity to use on the box -> put the switcher PROGRAM/ MANUAL on MANUAL -> put the switcher RUN/ADJUST on Adjust -> adjust current using the CURRENT ADJUST knob to the shock to use during the test (you can read the value on the SHOCK INTENSITY (mA) display) -> Switch the switcher RUN/ADJUST in the center

Export data Click Reports Select "Session ID" Click " Export to Excel File " and select on the Dialog window the file you want to export and click OK Select the folder where you want to save your file, name the file and click "Open" Example of excel file, 4-worksheet: Session Results -> Subject ID, trials, latency and results (avoided, escaped or No Response) are reported in this worksheet. Acclimation -> number of crossing during acclimation time. InterTrialInterval -> inter trial time (seconds) and number of crossing during this interval. Protocol -> info about your session and protocol are saved in this worksheet.
